scope.summary: Makes the stdio init-token JSON path discoverable and removes the uncompletable generic setup secure-stdio snippet.
out_of_scope: The full R1 external-client rig lane.
The full C9 onboarding-snippet truth fixture, which also covers the HTTP Authorization-header work owned by B10.

entry_points.name: literal stdio initialize frame fixture
entry_points.trace: `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --test c2_stdio_init_token_literal_frame -- --nocapture` exited 0 at d0263cb2 with 5 passed; it proves the literal params._meta["oraclemcp/initToken"] path authenticates, misplaced/absent tokens are Missing, mismatched tokens are Mismatch, and the exact path is documented.

entry_points.name: stdio init-token gate unit
entry_points.trace: `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp-core init_token_gate_rejects_missing_and_wrong_under_required -- --nocapture` exited 0 at d0263cb2 with 1 passed; it asserts the Missing error text carries params._meta["oraclemcp/initToken"] and says the value must be a JSON string.

entry_points.name: generic setup payload snippet contract
entry_points.trace: `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp --bin oraclemcp setup_payload_is_generic_and_client_ready -- --nocapture` exited 0 at d0263cb2 with 1 passed; it asserts setup omits secure_stdio and ORACLEMCP_STDIO_TOKEN from generic MCP snippets because mainstream clients cannot inject initialize _meta.

limitations: This close does not claim the full C9/R1 external-client rig lane; that fixture remains open and also covers B10's HTTP snippet Authorization header.
This close does not run a workspace-wide Cargo gate.
The shared checkout contains dirty files outside this bead's scope, so only the source-scoped tree cleanliness is claimed.
